title: Ensure end-to-end TLS encryption is enabled

input_row.title: Ensure end-to-end TLS encryption is enabled
input_row.description: End-to-end (E2E) TLS encryption ensures that front-end to worker communication within App Service apps is encrypted using TLS. Without this feature, while incoming HTTPS requests are encrypted to the front ends, the traffic from front ends to workers running the application workloads would travel unencrypted inside Azure’s infrastructure.
input_row.rationale: E2E TLS helps ensure full encryption of traffic between: • Clients and front ends • Front ends and worker processes hosting the application
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, ensure that Enable end-to-end TLS encryption is set to On. 5. Repeat steps 1-4 for each app. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list apps: az webapp list For each app, run the following command to get the end-to-end TLS encryption setting: az webapp show --resource-group --name -- query endToEndEncryptionEnabled Ensure that the command returns true. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: af1d7e88-c1c8-4ea8-be1f-87bff0df9101 - Name: 'App Service apps should enable end to end encryption'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, next to Enable end-to-end TLS encryption, click the radio button next to On. 5. Click Save. 6. Click Continue. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each app requiring remediation, run the following command to enable end-to-end TLS encryption: az resource update --resource-group --name - -resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" --set properties.endToEndEncryptionEnabled=true
input_row.impact: Enabling end-to-end TLS encryption may introduce minimal latency and require additional configuration of certificates and HTTPS settings to ensure compatibility.
input_row.default_value: By default, end-to-end TLS encryption is disabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
Given the context and the details provided in the JSON regarding end-to-end TLS encryption, the audit approach for this control is as follows:

**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_apigateway_api_end_to_end_tls_encryption_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- **AWS API Calls or boto3 SDK Methods:** 
  - Use the AWS API Gateway service to validate the configuration of TLS settings.
  - Specifically, AWS does not have a direct equivalent of Azure's App Service for end-to-end TLS but provides APIs for configuring custom domain names and managing the associated SSL/TLS certificates.
  
- **AWS CLI Commands (if applicable):**
  - The following AWS CLI command can be used to check the configuration of a custom domain associated with an API Gateway:
    ```
    aws apigateway get-domain-names
    ```
    This command retrieves all domain names configured in API Gateway, which can then be correlated with their TLS settings.
    
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - Custom domain names in API Gateway and their respective TLS configurations.
  
- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Check if the custom domain has an associated certificate that specifies TLS settings.
  - Ensure that the certificate used is valid (not expired, properly configured).
  
- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - Pass: Custom domain with TLS certificate is configured and not expired.
  - Fail: TLS certificate is missing, expired, or incorrectly configured.

This approach leverages AWS APIs and CLI tools to automate the verification of TLS settings prepared for potential similar functionality to that highlighted in the CIS controls for Azure.
